title: centropogon coccineus var. leucostoma
intro: Centropogon coccineus var. leucostoma, commonly known as a flowering plant, is a species in the family Campanulaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The therapeutic actions of Centropogon coccineus var. leucostoma include acting as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic agent, which can help treat wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

parts: Centropogon coccineus var. leucostoma has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers. The roots of the plant are known to contain alkaloids, including centropogonine and coccineine, which have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of Centropogon coccineus var. leucostoma contain flavonoids and terpenoids, including kaempferol and beta-sitosterol, which have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. side_effects: Centropogon coccineus var. leucostoma can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals.
